# Bayesian Evidence Score

A simple evidence score can be written as a likelihood ratio:

\[
LR=\frac{P(Data \mid Coordinated)}{P(Data \mid Independent)}
\]

Posterior odds:

\[
Posterior\ Odds = Prior\ Odds \times LR
\]

## Evidence streams

| Evidence stream | Raises likelihood of coordination when... |
|---|---|
| Timing | many accounts act within short windows repeatedly |
| Content | captions, clips, hashtags, or URLs are unusually similar |
| Actor history | accounts have narrow campaign-like histories |
| Network | dense clusters or core-periphery structures appear |
| Disclosure | commercial relationship is absent or hidden |
| External artefacts | briefs, payout pages, Discords, contracts, or regulator findings exist |

## Practical caution
A strong graph pattern with no external corroboration supports “coordination-like behaviour,” not necessarily “deceptive campaign.”
